crossEventQuestion: When should Microsoft fund a large all-cash acquisition with debt versus equity?
observation: For the $26.2B LinkedIn deal, debt was chosen to defer dilution and achieve accretion in under two years; for the $68.7B Activision deal, the all-cash structure was approved by both boards without explicit debt-vs-equity contrast in the announcement.
invariant: Large all-cash acquisitions are funded with debt to minimize near-term EPS dilution and accelerate accretion.
boundaryConditions: Acquisition must meet Microsoft's high bar for new additions
Expected close date influences dilution timeline
unresolvedConflicts: Activision deal does not explicitly state debt vs equity choice, only all-cash structure

decisionAxis.decisionQuestion: debt or equity for all-cash M&A

conditions: expected close date influences dilution timeline

conditionDelta: LinkedIn: $26.2B all-cash with explicit debt financing; Activision: $68.7B all-cash with board approval but no financing lever stated
